Paul Melendez.review_text: Avoid this professor at all costs. Narcissistic and does not care about the success of his students.

One of the best business professors at this school. Super cool guy, makes lectures entertaining and you are only graded by 4 one page papers, and 3 open note exams. I really like Prof Melendez and how he utilizes gvuest speakers which is refreshing as many other of my classes never did this. Great guy and cool class

Great professor and lecturer. You are graded on four memos and three exams. If follow the template you'll receive an A on the memos. The tests are not cumulative, they follow a certain set of modules, and they are open note. But there are things like case-studies that are not in the notes, so attendance is encouraged to have all the class material.

One of my favorite professors. Genuinely cares about the well-being of his students. Offers lots of extra credit for BNAD 302, which is an extremely test-heavy class. There are 300 points worth of exams (3x100) and 100 points worth of memo assignments (4x25). The exams can be difficult but are open-note. I'd advise copying the lecture slides for it

I took the 3 week version of this course so there was a lot of material to cover, but as long as you kept up with the weekly lectures, the workload was super manageable with just a weekly test and extra credit.  The only thing was that we were told we needed to purchase the textbook, but then didn't use it at all - all exams were based on lectures.

He is an amazing professor I loved him! Attendance is not mandatory however he does not post the slides so you have to go to class to get the material. However the lectures were interesting and easy to follow. You get as many notes as you want for exams so as long as you go to class and take notes you will get an A.

BNAD 302 is an easy class if you go to class and write down every word on the slides. Grade is made up of 3 open-note exams and 4 easy memo assignments based on guest lectures. He offers extra credit and makes his lectures funny. The only thing I didn't like was if you sit in the front he often touches you (hug, fist bump, knock on the shoulder).

Overall, this is a fairly easy class. Open note tests (notes have to be on paper/printed though), practically all you need to know on the test is covered in lecture (you can get by without the textbook), and he is very kind, engaging, and willing to answer questions during lecture.

Melendez's class is very simple. Write down every word of the lecture slides and you will ace the exams, which are open note -- they are the same word for word. He keeps his lectures interesting even in not-so-interesting topics and does care about his students. I would definitely recommend this class!

One of my favorite professors, so easy to listen to and learn from. There are 5 one page memos (Super Easy), 4 open-note exams on the class notes, and 2 extra credit opportunities. If you go to class it's an easy A. Never bought the textbook his lectures are all about it.

Professor Melendez is a decent professor and offers good extra credit opportunities but the class was exam heavy and I found the content to not be completely related to the textbook. With only 4 assignments and no attendance points it can be difficult to earn a good grade.

Victor Piscitello.review_text: Tells stories all class about his life- tells the same stories a million times. Doesn't teach in class at all but if you skip more than 2 classes and he catches you, he'll fail you. HW is easy. Tests are very hard no matter how much you study for them. Study guides don't help. Offers EC throughout the semester. 5 quizzes, drops 1.

He calls people out for being late and at times makes students feel uncomfortable with his comments. There are moments where he is funny, but there are others where he can kind of a Richard. I don't care for him.
Exams are really hard and specific. Test questions are so difficult.

There are those instructors who talk a lot in class but have no important points in lectures. For the online class, you have to do everything on your own. Read, do quizzes, and do exams. I don't feel like I learned something from the class, and had this class just for credit.
Be aware of pop quizzes and read the book. Lectures are all about his egocentric personal stories. Do the study yourself and you should be fine.

Easy to pass and do well if you do the homeworks, study for the exams, and go to class to get the participation! Vic is super helpful if you go to office hours or need any help. If his personality is annoying, tune him out during lecture- just take notes Tip: if you can do all the hws in the first two weeks, you don't need to buy the access code.

The class is challenging but not extreme torture. Put in effort and you'll pass. The biggest issue is Piscitello's personality. Some people love him but I don't think he realizes just how alienating and offensive he can be. I'm convinced half the class is only laughing along  because they feel they have no other choice. Not a good mentor.
His tests are 50 multiple choice questions about powerpoint slides you need to memorize. Long useless homeworks per week (1-2 hours). Very boring material, but tries to make class fun.
terrible class, terrible assistance. stay away unless you want to be confused and receive bad grade.

Hard A. Exams are too specific and are weighted too heavily. Gives extra credits but 90% of them are for sport games. Homework are easy but only worth 13%. The 4 quizzes that we had were worth 17% and there's only 5 questions per quiz. In class, he mainly talks about his life story and makes you remember random facts that'll be on the exam.

The class is difficult if you take it online. There are learn smart assignments and quizzes daily. He has 4 exams throughout the class and does not provide a study guide for any of them. Read the textbook if you want to do well on the exams because his powerpoint slides do not help at all. Make sure you do not fall behind. No extra credit.
Rants and tells pointless stories. Quizzes and tests are misleading. Easily the most annoying teacher I've had
sucks

Vic is one of the most engaging professors i've ever had. Always made class funny and gave lots of real-world perspectives on things. Exams weren't very difficult but also not easy. Class is mainly made up of exams, with HW/quizzes making up the rest. A really great guy that makes the class very interesting. Do your readings and HW and u'll be fine

I've heard that the BNAD 302 class isn't too bad. . IN PERSON. I've also heard from a previous student of Dr. Piscitello that he's admitted to making his online classes 3x tougher than his in person courses. The test are difficult in his online class but I don't think he has control over that--it's all through McGraw-Hill Connect (lots of money)
Funny guy, can cross the line sometimes. Decent teacher, it was a tough class, but he will hold your attention. Pay attention, keep quiet, and do NOT come in late.

Took his class online.Seemed very nice but he was very preoccupied being in Spain w/ his students so this may not be a good example.His online tests were fairly easy and straight forward. Pretty easy class overall, but again it might be because it was online, in class may be a different story.

Vic is by far the best teacher I've had at U of A. Class time passes by quickly because of his stories about his real life experiences. Not easy at all but not impossible to get an A..it's essential to go to class though! You wont mind going either because he is fun to listen to comapred to other profs...vic rocks!

Vic is the one of the best teacher's that I had at the UA. He is the certainly the most fair. If you go to class, study, and participate then you will do well. If you don't do those things then you won;t. simple as that. He also makes going to class almost fun, he's a cool guy and a great teacher.

Thought I wouldn't like this class within the first week, but over time it got better. He's tough on you and randomly selects names to ask questions for your attendance points. Quizzes were usually announced the class before or the day of. His teaching style is a bit aggressive, but I honestly learned a lot in his class and it kept my focused.

Never choose this professor. He very likes to call students names to get 5 points attendance. Pop quiz is pretty hard even you are prepared. He very likes to ask student question that let me feel so uncomfortable. You can try online class that easy A.
Awesome teacher. His class was my favorite of all classes I've taken at the UA. He is super funny. Cusses a lot. Gets students involved. Sit where he can get to know you.

Maybe he's not great for 361, but for this Business Minor class he was great! Lectures are entertaining (though pretty worthless overall). Tests are difficult, but manageable because he gives a solid study guide the class before. He also offered us significant extra credit. I'm so glad I've taken a class of his. He's actually worth our tuition $$$.

Vic is such an amazing professor! You have to come to lecture in order to get participation points, but lectures are always interesting because he gives real world examples that better help you understand the information. Super funny teacher, definitely recommend!

This was the first online class i took,  because it was a winter session class there was assignments almost every day,  but  he was always willing to help, very good at responding emails and telling us about due dates, VERY well organized. Excellent experience!!!! Great professor great class... definitely recommended!!i got a B

One of the best classes I have taken at the UA. I truly enjoyed the class and would recommend it to everyone. You have to work for your A. Best way to prepare is reading the book. Sounds awful, but the curriculum is interesting and you can get through it. Class is extremely fun and entertaining. If you need help, ask and vic will do so!!

Very helpful lectures, and he is very passionate about what he is teaching. He can hold your attention pretty well, and involves the class in his lectures. He makes the material (which is pretty easy anyway) very clear, and enjoyable.

Vic truly practices what he teaches and gives his students a live model for the real world. Entertaining and engaging, though some might find his style a little aggressive, I think he helps prepare students for the jungle that is professional life. Great teacher and enjoyed the class immensely, I learned a ton about leadership and managing people.

People who say that Vic is the worst professor they've every had are probably the bottom-feeders of school. If you show up to class (sometimes he randomly will have an easy quiz or do a participation grade when there aren't that many people) you'll be fine. His lectures are entertaining and help you remember key terms for the test. Great teacher!

Literally the best Professor I have ever had. He's so engaging and is actually one of the few professors that had a career before they started teaching. He knows what he's talking about. The class isn't hard if you go to lecture. If you skip you will fail. He's worth the attendance though. It's nearly impossible to be bored in his lectures.

Vic is one of the best professors I have had here. You can tell that he honestly cares about his students because he really doesn't need to be teaching. He was a millionaire in his 20s, so he clearly doesn't need the teaching salary. If you make a point to get to know him, he will make it a point to help you out. Go to class for the participation!
